understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the KA59 puzzle game by solving each level within its step budget, where a level is won only when every goal tile marked '+' is simultaneously occupied by selectable piece(s) (including handling levels with multiple goals by placing pieces on all of them at the same time).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) attempted to shift only the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction; movement could be blocked by walls/boundaries or by objects adjacent in that direction, and the movement.*_blocked flag was only a hint that something was adjacent and not a guarantee of failure. Some adjacent objects were pushable, so when moving into them the selected piece could push them if space/physics allowed, otherwise the move would have no effect. Levels could include multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with an exact pixel-coordinate target_position matching a selectable's position switched control to that piece, which was necessary to place multiple pieces onto multiple goal tiles at once.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the KA59 puzzle game by solving each level within its step budget, where a level is won only when every goal tile marked '+' is simultaneously occupied by selectable piece(s) (including handling levels with multiple goals by placing pieces on all of them at the same time).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) attempted to shift only the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction; movement could be blocked by walls/boundaries or by objects adjacent in that direction, and the movement.*_blocked flag was only a hint that something was adjacent and not a guarantee of failure. Some adjacent objects were pushable, so when moving into them the selected piece could push them if space/physics allowed, otherwise the move would have no effect. Levels could include multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with an exact pixel-coordinate target_position matching a selectable's position switched control to that piece, which was necessary to place multiple pieces onto multiple goal tiles at once.
